teamObjective.explanation: Opening lead with only low non-trump cards. The priority is to discard the weakest card and preserve any slightly higher cards for future tricks.

partnerBelief.summary: No information about partner's hand at the start of the game.

opponentBelief.summary: No information about opponent hands at the start of the game.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Play the lowest card (3 of spades) because the hand contains only weak non-trump singles. Leading a higher card would waste relative strength and make it harder to play the 3 later.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 4 is stronger than the 3; keeping it gives more flexibility later.
The 5 is stronger than the 3 and 4; better retained for later tricks.
The 6 is the strongest card in hand; leading it would sacrifice the most value.

risks: The 3 of spades is very likely to be beaten by any higher single or trump.
Partner may be unable to take control, allowing opponents to lead the next trick.
riskAssessment.mitigation: This risk is unavoidable with such a weak hand; minimizing loss by expending the lowest card is the best available strategy.

notes: No trumps, pairs, or combinations are available, so a single-card lead is mandatory.
